summary: Hybrid fixed+trail exit composition is research-integrity sound; the only flag is the taker_buy_ratio_5m field requiring the 5m window to be pre-T0, which is a verification item not a leakage finding.

risk_assessments.leakage_notes: novel_dim uses taker_buy_ratio_5m>=0.60. The naming suffix '_5m' is ambiguous: if it denotes the trailing 5m window ending at T0 (lookback) it is clean; if it denotes a 5m window starting at T0 (lookahead) it would be post-T0 and leak. Given the SUPPORTED_FIELDS enforcement post-R1 and that taker_buy_ratio_5m is part of the standard pre-T0 microstructure feature set, treating as low rather than block. Verification: confirm the field's window is [T0-5m, T0], not [T0, T0+5m].
risk_assessments.live_conflict_notes: Exit-side archetype with no entry directionality (channel=NA, side=NA); cannot generate positions that conflict with the live BWE_OI_Price pump-long monitor — it only governs exit composition for whatever entry it is paired with.
risk_assessments.remediation: Confirm in code review that taker_buy_ratio_5m is computed over the trailing window ending at T0 (not the forward window). If the implementation accidentally uses the forward 5m window, escalate to block — but the field name alone is not sufficient evidence of leakage given the post-R1 SUPPORTED_FIELDS gate.
